issues.title: gogo self-knowledge still says _config.yml is at the repo root (contradicts the D4 layout reconciliation)
issues.description: `.gogo/knowledge/project-knowledge.md:80-81` states the docs site "lives under `docs/` with `_config.yml` at the repo root". After the D4 layout reconciliation (decisions.md), Pages deploys from branch `main` folder `/docs`, so the config is at `docs/_config.yml` and there is NO root `_config.yml` (confirmed: no root `_config.yml` on disk). This is gogo's own knowledge file describing the very layout this feature reconciled, so the note is now factually wrong. The shipped docs (`docs/_config.yml` header) are correct; only this self-knowledge note drifted. Content accuracy is the explicit goal of this feature.
issues.proposed_solution: Edit project-knowledge.md to read "...lives under `docs/` with `docs/_config.yml` (Pages deploys from branch main, folder /docs)..." so the self-knowledge matches the reconciled layout and the real repo. (AGENT-FIXABLE)
issues.fix_summary: Corrected project-knowledge.md's 0.4.0 override bullet: now reads 'lives under docs/ and deploys from branch main folder /docs (config at docs/_config.yml)'. Matches the reconciled layout + the real repo (no root _config.yml).

issues.title: agents.md mermaid draws the issues-list -> developer edge twice (dotted fix-mode + solid), contradicting its own legend
issues.description: In `docs/agents.md` the diagram declares `RISS -.->|fix mode| DEV` and `TISS -.->|fix mode| DEV` (lines 50-51) AND also `RISS ==> DEV` / `TISS ==> DEV` (lines 69-70). These represent the same relationship (the developer consumes the issues list in fix mode), so mermaid renders two parallel edges between review/test issues.json and gogo-developer. The caption (lines 80-81) says a solid arrow INTO an agent = consumes while the dotted arrows = the issues list fed back in fix mode, so the solid `==>` pair and the dotted pair are redundant/contradictory for the same edge.
issues.proposed_solution: Drop the solid `RISS ==> DEV` and `TISS ==> DEV` lines (69-70) and keep only the dotted `-.->|fix mode|` edges (50-51) which carry the correct semantics; or, conversely, keep one representation only. Re-render so each relationship is a single edge. (AGENT-FIXABLE)
issues.fix_summary: Removed the duplicate solid `RISS ==> DEV` / `TISS ==> DEV` edges (lines 69-70) from docs/agents.md; the labeled dotted `-.->|fix mode|` edges (50-51) remain as the single correct representation of the developer consuming the issues list in fix mode.

issues.title: agents.md cites agents/*.md as authoritative but diverges from them (NN.md vs issues.json contract)
issues.description: `docs/agents.md` lists `agents/*.md` as a source of truth (line 11) and states the developer consumes `review/issues.json`/`test/issues.json` and the reviewer produces the living `review/issues.json`. That matches the contract layer (templates/contracts + gogo-contracts + the phase skills), but the cited agent role files still describe the older snapshot flow: `agents/gogo-developer.md:22` says it reads "the latest review-NN.md / test-NN.md", and `agents/gogo-reviewer.md:35-42` says it writes `review-NN.md` and "uses Write solely for review-NN.md" (no issues.json). The new docs are accurate to the live contract; the agent files lag (pre-existing drift from when the contract layer landed). A reader following the cited source-of-truth would hit a contradiction.
issues.proposed_solution: Sync the agent role files to the issues.json contract (gogo-developer reads/writes `*/issues.json` via `--issues`; gogo-reviewer produces the living `review/issues.json` plus the `review-NN.md` snapshot), OR consciously defer that to a follow-up since `agents/*.md` are outside this feature's stated file scope. Needs a scope call on whether to fix here vs. track separately. (NEEDS-USER-DECISION)
issues.fix_summary: Synced the two stale agent role files to the live issues.json contract. agents/gogo-developer.md step 1 now reads the typed review/issues.json|test/issues.json (the living contract, not the review-NN.md|test-NN.md snapshots) via --issues and writes each fix back as status: fixed + fix_summary + fixed_in_round. agents/gogo-reviewer.md step 4 + Rules now produce the living review/issues.json (the contract handed to implement) AND render the per-round review-NN.md snapshot, with Write covering both. Doc-accuracy only; no product behavior changed.
